key_insight: A self-supervised EEG transformer (STST-JEPA) pretrained on 47,703 sessions learned age-relevant brain signals well enough to deliver strong cross-dataset age prediction and competitive transfer to related EEG tasks.
clinical_relevance: This work suggests EEG foundation models could support scalable brain-age biomarkers and other screening applications, but they remain research tools rather than validated clinical decision aids.
method: Self-supervised transformer trained on resting-state and task EEG from ages 5–81.
Uses masked-token latent prediction against an EMA target plus an auxiliary reconstruction loss.
Learns from 30-second multi-channel windows with spatiotemporal block masking.
evidence_signal: A frozen-encoder attentive probe reached 3.06 years MAE (r = 0.924) on 3,367 held-out sessions, and light finetuning achieved top leaderboard performance on sex classification, age prediction, and psychopathology regression.
watch_item: Performance was shown on benchmark datasets and leaderboards, so real-world deployment may be limited by cross-site montage differences, domain shift, and the need for broader external validation.
paper_title: STST-JEPA: Shallow-Target Spatio-Temporal Joint Embedding Prediction Architecture For EEG Self-Supervised Learning
